Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a set of activities SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to create a temporal profile dictionary for pairs of activities that eventually follow each other in at least one process execution Note The pairs are for activities that appear in order in a trace not necessarily consecutive For example in a trace SS OP RC we consider SS OP SS RC etc

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to estimate the average time and standard deviation for each pair We must consider the process flow and typical delays in a supply chain for high tech electronics

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets think about the typical flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Supplier Selection SS to Order Placement OP This is usually quick maybe a few days But note in complex supply chains supplier selection might involve negotiations so it could take some time Lets assume it takes on average 2 days with a standard deviation of 1 day because sometimes its quick sometimes longer

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Placement OP to Receive Components RC This is the lead time from the supplier For high tech components it might take weeks Lets say average 14 days with a standard deviation of 3 days due to supplier reliability shipping etc

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receive Components RC to Quality Inspection QI Usually done soon after receiving maybe within a day Average 1 day standard deviation 0 point 5 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Quality Inspection QI to Component Assembly CA If components pass assembly starts soon Average 0 point 5 days standard deviation 0 point 25 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Component Assembly CA to Product Testing PT Assembly might take a few days Lets say average 3 days standard deviation 1 day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Product Testing PT to Packaging PK If testing passes packaging is quick Average 0 point 5 days standard deviation 0 point 2 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Packaging PK to Warehouse Storage WS Immediately after packaging it goes to storage Average 0 point 1 days a couple of hours standard deviation 0 point 05 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Warehouse Storage WS to Distribution DT This depends on orders and shipping schedules Might take a few days Average 2 days standard deviation 1 day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Distribution DT to After Sales Support AS After distribution support is ongoing but we consider the time until the first after sales activity This might be weeks after distribution Average 30 days standard deviation 10 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we also consider pairs that are not consecutive For example SS to RC This is SS to OP plus OP to RC so average equals 2 plus 14 days standard deviation equals square root of 1 squared plus 3 squared about 3 point 16 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS to QI SS to OP plus OP to RC plus RC to QI equals 2 plus 14 plus 1 equals 17 days stdev equals square root of 1 squared plus 3 squared plus 0 point 5 squared about 3 point 20 days We can compute for many pairs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: But note The process might not be strictly linear For example after Quality Inspection if components fail they might be returned and reordered but we are considering the successful path Also there might be loops but for simplicity we assume a linear successful path

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However the problem says eventually follow each other in at least one process execution In a linear process every activity eventually follows every earlier activity So for 10 activities we have 45 pairs But we are to include a representative subset We do not have to list all 45 but we should include enough to be representative and complex

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will include pairs that are consecutive and some that are separated by multiple steps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will use units of seconds The example used seconds Lets convert days to seconds 1 day equals 86400 seconds

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now for non consecutive pairs we can compute the average as the sum of the averages of the intermediate steps and the standard deviation as the square root of the sum of variances

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The standard deviation of the sum of independent random variables is the square root of the sum of the variances We assume the times are independent

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can also consider pairs that are further apart like SS to PT SS to DT etc

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem says a representative subset We do not need to list every possible pair but we should include enough to show the idea

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We have 21 pairs That should be enough
